So the first thing I want to talk about, and I think we're now moving over to section four of the book, yeah, section four, so three types of traffic. So the first thing we'll talk about again is coming back to this, right? Who, where, bait, where, okay? So we talked a lot about who is the person you ideally want to sell to, right? We talked a lot about that, figuring out your customer avatar, finding their Google image picture, getting a perfect description of whoever your dream client is, okay? We talked a lot about that. We talked a little bit about bait. Tomorrow's going to be a lot more about bait. So we talked about where, your back end program, where are you going to take this person to, where are you sending him to, where is your dream with them? So right now, the rest of today, we're going to be focusing on where, where are these people at? Because you can find those people, it makes this whole world, this whole, your whole marketing job a lot easier. And so when I first started understanding this, and I first got into this internet business, the hard thing is that if you look at, you look at what everybody's selling online, right? All the internet marketing gurus, they're selling a thousand of ways to drive traffic, right? You're just going to do it on Facebook, on Twitter, on Bubble, and there's like so much stuff. I think most people get so overwhelmed because they're trying to learn how to drive traffic from a thousand different sources, and they're just like, yeah, what do I do, right? And so for me, like, I, I'm not going to dig into the technical part, how to do it, but I want you guys to understand the concept behind it. Because for me, the second I, I grasped the concept, from that point forward in my life, I was always able to get traffic, okay? And so the strategy behind this is more important than the tactics of how you do it, okay? So when I'm doing this process, there's, there's a couple initial questions I ask myself. And the first one comes back to over here is who is my target market? Okay, we talked a lot about that, so I'm not going to beat that with a dead horse, but you got to, you really have to know exactly who that person is, okay? And how to make money market, the person that I, the, the who that I always kind of look at, it was me when I was a little kid. When I was a little kid, some of you guys have probably heard of this story before, but I used to, I literally would collect junk mail. I would go, and I used to get Small Business Opportunity Magazine. You guys, who here subscribed to that magazine? If you're not, you should seriously get a subscription. It's like the best magazine in the world. It's 140 pages, four pages of articles and 136 pages of ads. Yeah, yeah. And for dudes like me, I love it. Like the ads are like, that's the gold, right? You read everyone's headlines and how they write their ads. And so I literally was like 12 years old when I got my first copy, and that's a bad idea to give a 12 year old like small bizzare magazine, because you're reading and you're like, oh my gosh, oh, anyway, I read every single ad and I would always call the 800 number at the end and like, hey, can you send me your free kit? Hey, can you send me your free kit? And so eventually I was getting so many free kits in the mail from every opportunity in the world that the mailman would show up with the stack of mail so big it would not physically fit in my parents' mailbox. And so the mailman has to come up to the door, knock on the door, and hand my parents a stack of junk mail this big. And so I come home from school and like what was the seventh, eighth grade, I come home from school and there'd be a big stack of Russell's junk mail and then my parents have like one or two letters. I'd be so excited to grab this big stack of junk mail, I'd go in my room and I'd open it. I'd be reading sales letters after sales letters and that's probably where I got into this whole thing from. But I just got addicted to that, that concept, right? And I read these things, I remember some of them I'm like, oh, that's dumb. Oh, that's dumb. And some of them I'd be like, oh my gosh, I got to do this. I got to like ask my parents, can I borrow 100 bucks? I need 100 bucks to be able to do this. Like they would sell me on the next thing. And so whenever I'm trying to create like an opportunity type product, I think back to like when I was this little kid and I was struggling and I wanted to make money so bad. And I think like would that have been something that I would have like been attracted to? That's what I always look at. So for you guys, think about like whenever it was you got into whatever you do, like what was the things that attracted you to that? Okay. And if you think about that, that's what's going to really, that's why I decided if it offers good or bad. Okay. And then we're on our weight loss stuff. I think about like when I need to lose a lot of weight, like what were the things that like without have got me to get up and to move? So yeah, the first thing is just really understanding who your person is. And then second thing is understanding where, where those people are at. Okay. Those are the two key questions of traffic. Who is my target market? Where are those people at? Okay. For example, probably the best way to illustrate this is, is when you hear word congregation, what do you guys think of? Church, right? So every Sunday, all the people that have similar beliefs and values will congregate together, right? So all the Mormons, we congregate together in certain churches, right? All of the Baptists congregate over here and the Catholics congregate over here. Okay. On Saturday, the seventh day of venice are over here. Okay. Everyone's congregating together based on similar beliefs and values and ideals and they're hanging out together. Okay. Now when I understand that people are congregating, if I'm a marketer and I want to sell something, let's say I'm selling something, like for, for the Mormon world, there's these guys that sell these things called living scriptures, these DVDs they made of, of like all the Mormon stuff, right? Where do you sell it? Like where's the existing congregation? They're right there. They're hanging out together every Sunday. If you're selling something like that's where they're at. Okay. If you're selling something to the Baptists, they're, they're, every week they're together, congregate, like that's where they're at. Just go sell, like they're right there. Go. Right. When I understood that, I started thinking about that. My goal is not to try to tell you guys to go to churches and sell stuff. I know you could. But my goal is to help you understand the power of congregation because before the internet, people couldn't congregate together. Okay. The power of the internet is that people all of a sudden can start congregating together based on their beliefs and their values. For example, if you're a wrestler, and I know this because when I would wrestle with Boise State, every night we all had mandatory study hall for two hours because almost every wrestler flunked out of school, every single, I don't know what is with the wrestlers, like we are dumb people, like, like literally, so we, they forced us to go to two hours of study hall every single night. And so study hall, they hit up into computers and you watch the entire wrestling team come up there. We'd sit down and guess what? Every single one of us went to the exact same website. We went to the mat.com. We, there was a message board and for two hours every single night, every single one of us sat there and we talked on the mat.com to have their wrestlers. And this was happening at every single university in America. It's happening every single high school. It's happening like anyone who cared about wrestling every night was on the mat. Am I right, Brandon? Everyone's there, right? So if I'm going to be selling something to wrestlers, where am I going to look at? The mat.com, they're all congregated together, they're sitting there all day, all night talking about wrestling. That's where my, that's where they're at. So all I got to do is throw my bait out into that pond and start pulling people in. Okay. Think about the things that you're passionate about. Okay. If you're in a personal development, what sites do you go to? Where are you congregating? Okay. If you're into real estate, where are you congregating at? Okay. Think about your target customer. Where are they at? Where are they hanging out? What blogs are they reading? What email newsletters are they on? What Facebook groups are they part of? What, you know, like, I start going through all these different things. And as soon as I know that, like, they're all on Facebook and they're all on this fan page. Okay. If I'm selling a juicing product, Drew Canole's got 1.1 million people who are interested in juicing sitting there, congregating, talking about juicing. Hmm, where should I go to sell my juicing product? Shailoa, there's a site with a million people talking about juicing all day long. Boom, there they're at. I'll tell you for now, a little extra money. There's tons of them, though. And it's not just Drew Canole's. There's another guy. There's probably a thousand sites now based on it. But that's the power of the internet, guys. That's why little guys like us can make insane amounts of money, because we don't have to spend money driving TV and all these kind of things. When we were doing consulting stuff with Tony Robbins, what was interesting is he said, he told me, he said, Russell, when I first started this business, he said there were three channels. It was ABC, NBC, and CBS. That was it. Everyone was watching one of the three channels. So guess what I did? I bought ads in all three. And we made millions of dollars. And all of a sudden, all these cable shows started popping up. And suddenly there was like a thousand different channels. He was like, I could not afford to advertise. Everyone was every, and it was too expensive to acquire customers. And we had to cut everything. And so he was freaking out. Like, I can't grow my business because it's so expensive because there are everything out there. So he started shifting his marketing online. You know, online is powerful because I can only show my message. It's like, if I'm on, if I buy an ad in the Super Bowl and I'm selling my, how to make a potato gun product, okay, there may be 18 bajillion people watching it, but there's only three guys who want to buy my potato gun product. Okay, unless I'm selling a thing for like $800,000 a piece, I'm not going to make my money back. Right? But I can go online and find a potato gun group with 20,000 people who are rabid talking about potato guns. And they're sitting in one little congregation hanging out. I just throw my bait in the water and start pulling out money. Boom, all day long, all day long, right? That's what traffic is, you guys. It's not SEO, PPC, all this crap that, that, all these terms that everyone's coming up with. And that's the, the tactics behind how you do it. But none of you guys as entrepreneurs should know how to do that. Who here thinks I know how to run a Facebook ad? I'm scared to death of Facebook. I told you earlier, I never, ever, ever, ever, ever log in. We're making sane amounts of money off Facebook. Okay, who here thinks I know how to buy an email ad? I don't know how to, but I don't. Okay? Your job as entrepreneurs is not to understand the tactics behind it. There's geniuses who do the tactics all day long and you can hire them for pennies on the dollar. Your job as an entrepreneur is to understand very, very clear exactly who your person is and where they at. And after you know that this is where they're at, now we've got to figure out how to get our bait out in front of them. Now I can hire a guy to do it or I can figure it out. It doesn't matter, but they're right there. That's what they're all, they're sitting right there. It's like shooting what? Shooting fish in a barrel, is what they call it? Ducks in a barrel? I don't know. But they're all right there. Okay? Do you guys, do you guys get that? That's the power of this, this whole internet thing. That's why people like me who are not that intelligent can make millions and millions of dollars because I know two things. I know who my target market is and I know exactly where they're at. So your job as entrepreneurs is to be the master of these two things. Understand this person better than anyone else in the world knowing exactly where they're at. And the last step is where you can take these people. Okay? This is all the value ladder stuff. Now that I got them, I need to get bait to get them in the door and let's take them. Where can I take these guys to? How can I help them? How much more value can I kind of provide them? Because the more value I can provide them, the more money I'm going to make. And the more help I'm going to give to them. It's really, isn't that kind of a cool process when you understand that? Okay? So quit worrying about all the, that's why on this event you will never learn the tactical parts of it. Because the tactics change every single day and it's, you're always chasing and you're running and it's just, that's not the focus. The focus of entrepreneurship is to do this part. Okay? If you're going to ask a building contractor, you're like, hey man, so tell me how you, how you put all the electrical wires in. You can look at it and be like, I hired an electrician. Right? You're like, well, okay, that makes sense. Well, how do you, how do you do all of the foundation stuff? Hire the foundation guy. Ah, okay. That makes sense, you guys. The contractor doesn't know how to do all that crap. Guess what he knows how to do? He knows how to contract it out. Guess what I know how to do? I know where my people are at. I just contract it out. Hey, they're right there. Go get them. Sweet man. There's some more over there. Oh, I found this cool Facebook page. Oh, I just do what I'm doing all day long and I find cool places and then I have my team. I have people in place and just say, hey, go get them. They're there at boom and we start going after them. Okay. That's the one for you guys as well. Okay. It should not be, and there's things you're going to have to learn, especially when you're getting started. Obviously, you got to do all this yourself. But the reality is if you get so caught up in the technical part, and I did this, I can't tell you how many times I got caught up trying to learn SEO and man, I went on like this three year SEO binge where I thought it was the coolest thing in the world and I was studying stuff and we were like, we spent so much time and energy money, we built teams of people and I don't think we ever made a penny off of like, like literally got so tied up in the tactics. And then Facebook came around and I was like, there's my people and we hired someone like, hey man, can you just go get them and boom, leads start coming like crazy. I was like, that was way easier. So much easier. And so that's what I really want to stress for you guys is that process. Okay. All right. So that's the first is who and where. The second thing is you have to understand there's three types of traffic. Okay. And this is another one of the big keys, the big ahas that I had. And I remember I had this, I was like, wow, it's all becomes really, really easy now. Okay. The first type of traffic is traffic you control. Okay. So I have this traffic. I control it. So an example of traffic I control is a Facebook ad. I don't own that traffic. Facebook does, right? This is Facebook's people that are on Facebook, but I can buy an ad so I can control that traffic and say, hey, go right there. Okay. I can go to Google and buy a paper click ad. Google owns that traffic. It's there, it's their people, but I can buy an ad. I can control that traffic and send it over here. Okay. Um, I can go, somebody else has an email list. I have a million people that I know are my target market. I can go to that guy and say, hey, we buy an ad and send it over here. So he goes and he sends it over here. So I don't own that traffic, right? But I control it. So paid ads is traffic we control. We can, we can pay money and tell people where to push that traffic. Okay. That's traffic you control. Typically you're always going to push traffic you control through something we call a squeeze page. We'll talk about here in a minute. The second type of traffic is traffic you don't control. Okay. Um, a couple examples that is like, um, SEO. Okay. Anybody here who tried to do SEO? Hope you guys, I'm here to love SEO. Nobody loves it, especially after the last year or two or three. Anyway, um, uh, so SEO is traffic. I don't really control. I do a bunch of stuff and I hope for stuff, but like people just randomly show up or they don't show up. I'm not controlling. I'm just, I'm hoping and praying. I'm doing a bunch of stuff. I'm a good, probably the best example of this is I remember, uh, this is probably five or six years ago, um, back when BJ was working for me doing my SEO, um, back when we were trying to figure it out. And, um, one of my friends, Willie Crawford had written it, had written an email saying, and the title of his email is Russell Brunson is a little snot. I remember like, it really was like, yeah, it's kind of funny. So I copy his emails, posted on my blog and I put it up there and didn't think much about it. And two days later, BJ comes to my office kind of laughing. He's like, you want to see something funny? I'm like, yeah, what's that? Is it going to Google type in little snot? I'm like, why is it just, just go try it. I would check it out. So I type in little snot and guess who's ranked number one in Google for the keyword little snot. I was my blog was number one on Google for little snot. And I was so proud of myself. Now, what's interesting is like, there's probably someone somewhere. Maybe probably not. But if somebody happened to go to Google and type in little snot, and all of a sudden they see my site and they click on it, they show up my site. That person got to my site. I didn't control them. I have no idea what made them wake up that morning and type in little snot, but it worked and they showed up my site. Okay. That's an example of traffic you don't control. Another good one is, is a lot of Facebook, they paid Facebook ads. I'm controlling it. A lot of the other stuff happens on Facebook. I don't control that. For example, I write something funny on Facebook and then Brandon makes fun of it and then his friend shares it and somehow somebody shows up my site and I don't know how that'll happen. But they just show up and it's just traffic I don't control. All right. But typically traffic I don't control. I send them to like my blog. Okay. Now, both of these kinds of traffic things are good, but the, my only, my only goal with traffic I control and traffic that I don't control is to convert those people from those types of traffic into traffic that I own. Traffic that I own is the best type of traffic. That's what I want. Cause when I own that traffic, I don't got to pay for it again. It's my traffic. I can do whatever I want with it. I can send it here. I can send it here. I can read an email one day where Joel, Terry and is like, Hey, we're giving away a Ferrari to whoever you sell the most stuff. I'm like, awesome. Hey traffic over there. Boom. I got a Ferrari. What's the next thing? Right. You can sell whatever you want. You own that traffic. You can push it wherever you want. And that's the goal. Okay. This is how you become financially free is by owning traffic. Okay. And the first time I think I really got this, I was, I was reading, I was reading an article about some guy had posted online. I wish I, I wish I still had this article. It was like the biggest aha for me. We was talking about, he said, I'm sure you guys have read those articles from the gurus about how I made $25,000 in an hour and how I did all these things. And you probably thought it was a big scam, right? I was like, yeah, I've shared it. Like who makes 25 grand an hour? There's a scam. And he wrote back to like, it's actually not. Let me tell you how it works. He said these big gurus, they go out there and they spend all this time and energy and money. They build up a big, huge email list. Okay. E-mail list. Let's say they have an email list of 100,000 people. It says one day they wake up and they decide to promote a product. They send an email out to that product. And let's say they just get 1% of the people to buy that product. Let's say it's a $50 product. What's the math on that? 50K. They're like, okay, well, let's just say, let's say only point 1% of people actually buy that. That's 5K. I was like, 5K? That's how much I'm making three months right now. I'm sending out email to my list. So I had this, my big epiphany. I'm like, this is awesome. I got to get me an email list. How am I going to do that? So I started researching an email list, email list. And I found this website. It's called spamfreemailaddresses.com. And they're selling these DVDs with like 4.3 million people's email addresses. I'm like, dude, this is awesome. Like, like, I get it. Load them up, send them out email. If I get 0.001%, I'm rich. Game over, right? Listen to how my young naive mind is working. So I buy the DVD, spend $50 on this spam. I mean, this mailing software to make the emails go out. I load it up, load up the 4.3 million email address. I write a little ad and I click send. This is back in the day as a dial-up mode. It's my dial-up mode. It's plugging the wall. And I tell my wife that night, she's supporting me while I'm going to school, paying the bills. I said, honey. I was like, 4.3 million. I'm showing the math. I'm very, you know, have my glasses on. 4.3 million dollars. I'm like, if we only get 0.0001% to buy this, we'll be millionaires in the morning. And but I don't think that we're, you know, let's be conservative. Let's say it's 0.0001. It's a hundred grand. You know, I do all the math and I'm like, worst case scenario, we make ten grand by morning. So you're going to quit your job if you want. She's making like a thousand bucks a month at a time. And she's like, okay, like this is legal, right? I'm like, oh yeah, everyone's doing it. It's sad these guys make their money. So load the whole thing up and go to bed. Tell her to quit her job in the morning. Emails are being sent out all night. I'm having like daydreams of like mansions and everything great that's going to happen to me, right? And I wake up in the morning and she's like, I got to use the phone. I'm like, you can't like, we're, like we're becoming rich. Like emails are going out. You can't just use the phone. She's like, I have to call this person. I'm like, ah, because back then you guys remember when you had dial-up modem? You had like unplug your computer and plug back in your phone. So I get on the desk and I unplug the modem and I plug back in the phone and literally I'm still in the desk. I plug it in and I hear the phone ring. I'm like, oh, that's weird. So I come back out and pick up the phone and this person is cursing me out on the other line. I'm like, what's going on? And my wife and she comes out like waiting for the phone. She sees my face kind of drop and I'm like, I'm so sorry. I'm so sorry. No, I didn't know. Oh really? Oh crap. Okay. I'm so sorry. And so she's freaking like, what's happening? What's happening? And I hang up and I'm like, yeah, they just shut down our internet access and they're threatening to sue us. And she's like, what's going on? I'm like, I thought you said it was legal. I'm like, I swear it is. And anyway, so we go through this whole thing and my internet's completely shut down. She goes to work and I'm like, please, on the way out, please, please, please, please don't quit. Like give me a couple hours to cool this off and then you can, yeah. So I'm trying to like, I'm trying to see what happened, but my internet's gone. So I'm kind of screwed. I'm still going to school. So I put my backpack on and, you know, start, start walking to, walk into class. I get to class, I'm listening to my teacher talk and I'm just bored out of my mind. And all of a sudden I'm like, there's a computer lab next door. I gotta see what's happening. So I get off my, and I sneak out. This is my, anyone still in college? Can you teach this to your kids? This is like the secret, right? So if you hate your teacher and you're bored, you just go like this and kind of start squirming a little bit and you get this concern that you can just walk in and they're like, oh yeah, and you walk out like you're going to the bathroom and then you walk right into the computer lab. So I used to always do that. So I just did that. Like take out to the bathroom, walk out, go into the computer lab and check my emails and look at some stuff. And I decided to log in my PayPal account. I was selling these things for PayPal. I logged in my PayPal account. I had to make like nine cells during the night. And I was like, holy crap. It worked. Kind of. I started thinking, I'm like, okay, I was rewarded for this. It worked. People were buying this thing. So it's, it works, but I did it wrong. Like, I say, if I can figure out how to do this legitimate way, that's not going to be shut down. This could be the greatest thing in the world. And so that began my mission. Like how do I, how do I build a list? Okay. But you guys, the reality of this, this concept is true. Um, for most of you guys, you're going to, some of you guys have lists, some of you guys don't know, but if you're one of your main folks, one of your main KPIs, we talked about earlier today, your main things you're focusing on the size of my list, how many people are my adding each day? That's when your main core focuses. It'll keep growing and keep growing. The first you'll get a hundred people and then 500 and a thousand and 10,000 and 20 and 30. And somewhere along the line, you're going to get the point where literally, um, anytime you want to get paid, you just wake up, you write an email, you click send, you go back to bed and cash comes in. Who here has experienced that in this room? How does it feel today? He did it. He felt, and does it not feel like the greatest thing in the world? You feel like a rock star? It's the coolest thing in the world. And that's like really the, the internet dream, right? Is that. And so for, for us, we've got to think about how do we, how do we make this happen without me doing the legal thing and getting shut down? Cause I can teach you guys how to spam. If you want, we try it for a little while. It's not very much fun, but there's a legitimate way you can do it. That's long-term that'll, that'll feed you for the rest of your life. Literally almost every single time, like our company is collapsed, which has happened multiple times. Um, the thing that saves every single time is our email list. We come back to it like, all right, send an email out and boom, it saves us every single time. Like it's, it's the thing that, uh, everything I have in my business, you take everything away, that would be the one thing I would keep because, um, I keep coming back to it and it just, it, it pays for everything. And so that's kind of the key. So the, the focus has to be you guys as we're doing all this traffic stuff. Again, just like this chart here, all the traffic I control, I'm sending it to a squeeze page. Okay. I'll talk more about squeeze pages tomorrow. And most of you guys probably know what that is. But if you don't know what it is, all this is a simple page where somebody gives me your email address, they click submit, and then they're taken to a page where they can buy something. Right. So I put them through this, this filter first so that when people are coming through here, percent of the people will join my list. Now they're on my list. Now I own that traffic. I can do whatever I want with them in the future. Okay. Traffic I don't control. I typically are sending them to my blog or something, but if you look at my blog, you know, it's the top one third of my blog. All it is is like a glorified squeeze page. I've got a video right here and I asked them to opt in. Cause I don't care if they type little snot. They'll see that way down here. I just want to get them on my email list. That's the only goal I have. Right. So all the traffic I don't control, all my, my Facebook stuff, my, all these other things I'm doing, I push them like, hey, go to.comseekers.com. I push them there cause I don't control them. When they show up there, they're on my list. Now I own them. Now I got them. I can sell my free book. I can get them in my value ladder. I can come to events like this. I guarantee all you guys somehow got into one of my funnels somewhere and that's how come you're here today right now. Okay. And so that's kind of what we're focusing on is, is all the traffic you control, you push through a squeeze page traffic. You don't control it through a blog. The goal again is to get their email address and now you own that traffic, traffic that you own. And now that's, that's the goal guys. That's the game of internet marketing. Okay. And like I said before, I think it's the next slide actually. The way we do that is, is kind of through this process. Back in the day, a lot of times traffic was a lot easier. So I would set up a squeeze page and I would drive traffic and I would pay whatever I would pay, not that much. And then over here, I would sell something over here and, and it was really easy to, to, to do it because there weren't a lot of people doing it and it was a lot cheaper. And so I could, I could make that work really, really, really easy. But then what happened is like all these people like you guys wanted to play in this game too and other people and ever made it hard and everyone shot made ad prices go up because everyone was playing right. So everyone starts doing this stuff. So all of a sudden it got more expensive to, to drive ads. Now my goal in every single funnel we do, which is, you know, again, we're doing all day tomorrow is I want to create a funnel where I put a dollar in ads in and immediately I make a dollar back out. Okay. And why do I do that? Cause those people just got converted from traffic. I controlled went through this process. I broke even now, but my goal is to pay money right here, break even right here. Now they're on my list. Now I can sell my other stuff. Right. So this is, this is the filter. They can't just drive through squeeze page now and break even. I drive through squeeze page, go through my initial funnel and make money. So when I first did it, we would, we would just have like one simple product that we would sell it and it would work. Put a dollar in, we get a dollar, dollar, $52 back out the back. Okay. And we get a customer. That was what we were doing. But then it got a little harder cause everyone was buying ads and got more expensive. And so what we did is we started figuring out different ways to make this work better. So what I would do is like someone would buy this product and then I would go and I'd add an upsell. Before I add the upsell, I put a dollar in and I make like 50 cents back out. I'm like, ah, this is a losing campaign. I'm not going to make any money. I had an upsell in there and all of a sudden I make more money. And now I get the point where I put in a dollar and I get a dollar back out. And it works again, right? And so this is kind of this, this funnel game is, is you build a funnel, you drive some traffic and your goal is to put a dollar in, you get a dollar back out. Okay. If it's less than that, then I got to change something. I had to add an upsell or tweak this or change this till we get a point where you put a dollar in, you get a dollar back out. And now I got a funnel I can do to start growing my list as rapidly and as quick as I can. Okay. And our business, that's all we're doing is building this funnel till it breaks even, drive leads in, and now we've got these leads we can sell anything we want to in the future. Okay. And so tomorrow, that's what the whole focus tomorrow is this funnel. I'm going to show you guys a ton of different iterations of how to build these funnels and what the best way in the, and just a ton of ideas. Cause that, this is where, this is where the game wins. As soon as you learn to build a funnel, you can lease, break even on game over. That's when you win this game. That's when your list starts growing as fast as you want. You can have a thousand people a day to your list. It's growing, growing, growing, and you can print money for the rest of your life. You guys like that? You guys want to be there? Okay. It's a, it's a, it's a good place to be. So our goal is to lease, break even. Obviously I want to make a profit on that front end. If I make a profit, like typically like the first time we put out there, if we send something out and it breaks even, cause like, as you know, what we like to do is all this weird split testing stuff we talked about here. If my initial test breaks even, we're like having a party, like, oh my gosh, we haven't even tested anything that's breaking even, made a couple of tweaks and also when we double our money, and now we're making, we're making profit on the front end, then it's just like a party every single day. So that's what we want to be at. But even if you just break even, okay, even if you just break even, that's a million dollar your business. And any market you want, almost not every market, in most of the good markets, it's, it's game over. Okay. Cause as soon as you break even, you're building that list and you can just, I mean, if I, if I was, uh, if I was to give you, or you give me a dollar, I give you $2 back off and you do that. Much as you can, right? That's when the other day asked me, what's your advertising budget? I'm like, it's a bajillion dollars a day as long as it's profitable. Like what do you want me to say? Like if you can turn a million dollars into $5 million a day, I'll do it. I'll give you a, I'll give you a trillion dollars a day. Like if you can turn it back out, right? So soon, and that's, that's what you have to understand. As soon as you get this thing to work, then it doesn't, it doesn't, there's no such thing as an advertising budget because you're breaking even. You put a dollar in on Monday, the ad goes out, Tuesday, the merchant account gets some money, Wednesday it's in your bank account and you start it over. Okay. So your ad budget disappears because you just, just crank it up. Okay. Look at our supplement, NuraCell. Um, we, we spent almost a year trying to get their funnel to work right. We, we had some variations. And finally in January this year, it worked. In January, we, we, uh, we spent 20, or we made $20,000 in NuraCell. I'm like, ah, that's awesome. Okay. Um, last month, which was March. So two months later, we made $300,000 in NuraCell. Okay. Guess why? Cranked it up. Converts, man. Just crank it up. How much media can we buy? Just keep buying it, keep buying it, keep buying it because it works. Like just, let's just keep doing it. Okay. Next month, you know, we're half a million. I haven't, if much media is I can buy successfully, I'm going to keep buying it, buying it, buying it because it's all, it's free. There's no budget. Okay. That's how, that's how you win this game. That's how you win this game big at a big level. Okay. You guys see what went through value ladder and all these other pieces because it's so key to understanding this part of it. When we understand this, the whole game becomes, becomes super, super easy. When we come back, I'm going to share with you guys, probably one of the coolest things. Okay. Because like I said, tomorrow is all about building a funnel, but I want to make it so when you start building your funnel, you know exactly what's going to work every single time because then you almost always succeed. So I'm going to show you guys a couple of tools we use to reverse engineer what's already working out there. And then we just model and clone what's working and boom, like you're in profit day number one. Okay. Who here wants to see that? It's exciting. Okay. And it's, as soon as I learned this, I was like, this is the stupidest, easiest thing in the world. You literally put a website in, it'll show you the entire sales funnel. It'll show you what sites they're buying ads on. It'll show you where all these congregations are at, everything we're talking about. In literally five, 10 minutes, I can show you almost every offer where all those things are at. Okay. So I'm going to show you guys that stuff. And then tonight you can go and you can play with some of those tools and you'll know exactly where your, where your target markets are at, what they're buying, what prices they're paying and makes the whole job a lot easier. So that's the game plan. Now you guys kind of understand a lot of the conceptual stuff behind traffic. That last session kind of helped a lot of you guys, hopefully. And so you don't have to be as smart as people probably think I am because I'm not. That's not the key. The key is really to understand is the entrepreneur where these things are at, who your market's at, where they're at. So now I'm going to help you guys to really get more specific and find out exactly where those guys are at. Okay. And this is where it starts getting really, really fun for me. So I had a, one of my friends and mentors, when I first got started in this business, he told me, he said, the thing about internet marketing is like everyone wants to figure out how to create traffic. Because like your job's not to create traffic. Traffic's already out there, right? People are already on Facebook and on these blogs and they're already everywhere out. You know, your goal is not to create new traffic. It's to find out where that traffic is at and then tap into it, figure out how to get that traffic and divert it back over to you. So when you heard that, it made a lot of sense to me. And that's kind of where this whole concept comes from. So the first thing I want to find out, what I'm going to do instead of me just going out, because what normally happens for most people, it still shocks me because I went the opposite way. When I got started, one of my big mentors, the guy that I looked at all the time was the guy named Armand Moran. Anyone here know Armand or seen his stuff? So Armand was like one of my first like heroes, right? One of the first attractive characters online just captured me. I was like, this guy's awesome. I was watching what he did. He was creating all these little software products. He called them generator software. So we had ebook generator, ecover generator, header generator, sales letter generator, all these different generator softwares and he had all these things. So I was trying to create my very first offer, my very first product. I was like, I want to make a software product. And my first idea was for a product called ZipBrander. And ZipBrander was just this little software product. It did one thing and I was so excited. I went and I found this guy in Romania. I paid him $20. He built the whole thing for me and I had my very first product. And I was like, well, how am I going to sell this? Well, I wasn't smart enough to just figure out how to go sell it. But I knew Armand stuff and I looked at all his sites and his sites looked awesome. So I just looked at the site and I modeled it exactly. My site looked identical to his. He had this little ecover image, I didn't even cover image. He had a header graphic, I had a header graphic. He had a sales letter and had like 13 paragraphs, mine had 13 paragraphs. I modeled it identical. And that was how I created my very first offer. And luckily for me, Armand knew what it was doing. So when I modeled him, it just, mine luckily worked as well. It was like this great thing. And I think a lot of us, for whatever reason, we don't model things. We just kind of think, oh, I'm going to try this and try stuff. And it just doesn't work, right? Tony Robbins went out to his, I can't remember which one of his events. We talked about it. He said like, if you want to become the best in the world at something, he's like, you don't go out and try to figure out on your own. You find the best person in the world and you find them and you model them and you do exactly what they do. And I've seen Tony, I've seen guys in his organization who do this. One of Tony's main trainers is a guy named Joseph MacLendon. And Joseph is awesome. The first time I met him, I was speaking at an event. And I did this close. It was one of my best pitches I ever did. I did my close and rushed to the back and he bought the thing. And afterwards he pulled me aside and he was like, man, your pitch was awesome. Just so you know, I'm going to model it identical for my next thing. I was like, I was like so on and I'm like, well, thank you very much. And then I saw him like a year or two later at a UPW. He'd come behind stage when he was presenting and we hung out in the like the little green room they have back there. And it was really cool. And he said, he told me, he's like, you know what, Russell? He's like, the way you did your pitch, he's like, I just modeled that and it worked. And so we started talking about modeling. He's like, man, he's like, I will never in a million years step into any, like try to do anything without finding the best person, just copying him. He's like, for example, he's like last like three months earlier, um, uh, I had this new product I was going to launch. And so I knew this guy named Jeff Walker, who does his product launch thing. And so he's like, I went to Jeff's site. I looked at his process. I saw the very first video was 38 minutes and 12 seconds. So I made a video was 38 minutes and 12 seconds. He said, I want to be to the second exactly the same on to model it. My upsell, the second video was this. And he's like, I modeled the look of his page, the fill of his page, the, the, the script, what he talked about. I talked about the equivalent for my things. Like I modeled as close as humanly possible because I knew this work. So why would I go out there and try to reinvent the wheel? I know this works. Let me just model it as close as I can for my stuff. And it's going to be successful. And he did, and he launched it and made a ton of money. Okay. So many of you guys out there, I promise you are trying to reinvent the wheel. Okay. And that's not the key in this, in this game. The game is not to ever reinvent the wheel. It's to find what's already working perfectly and then model it. Okay. All right. So this is how we do this process. Okay. And this is the key. This is why I want to do this session tonight before we get into funnel building because I do not want you to look or even try to build a funnel until you see, see your competitors funnels and, and so you can model it exactly. Okay. And so this all starts first off on, on where, where are, um, uh, where are your competitors? Okay. Who here knows who their competitors are in this online? Who here does not know who their competitors are? Okay. This is key, you guys. The first thing you have to know is who are your competitors? Who's selling the same thing you want to sell? Okay. If nobody is selling the exact same thing you want to sell, it's probably a bad sign. Okay. I get people all the time who are like, guess what, Russell? The coolest thing I found this market, nobody's in it. I'm the first dude in it. I'm like, don't be in it. Why not? Cause that means like, if nobody's in it, there's a good reason why. Other people are like, oh, I want to do a weight loss product. But guess what? Everyone's got a weight loss product, so I don't want to be in it. No. If everybody's doing a weight loss product, probably a good idea. It means there's a big, huge market, right? And so I want to look at it. So if you don't know who your competitors are, you need to find your competitors. That's one of the most important key things. Okay. Cause you want to see everything about them. I want to find out what they're selling, what price points they're selling, what their upsells down, what their downsells are. I want to see what traffic sources I want to see everything about them. And then I'm just going to model it. Okay. So if you don't know your competitors are yet, you need to find them. I'm going to show you guys how to find them. But that's the key. You got to find out who your competitors. Now, there's two type of competitors I'm looking for. First are my direct competitors. Okay. So if I have a weight loss offer, who are all the other people that have a weight loss offer? Okay. I want a direct competitor. So if I'm selling an ebook, who else is selling a weight loss ebook? I want to see, I need to know every single one of them. Okay. So that's a direct competitor. An indirect competitor is I got a weight loss ebook. Who are the people selling weight loss supplements? Who are the people selling weight loss magic creams? Who are the, who are the people that are also selling, uh, maybe fitness things, things that aren't identical, but are, but are close. Okay. So I'm looking at both those. Who are my direct competitors who have almost identical same product? And then who are my indirect competitors? Okay. And that's the, that's the first step here. Okay. Now the second step is after I, I know exactly who these competitors are. Um, the second step is I got to find out what it is they're doing. I want to, I want to reverse engineer what they're doing so I can model it. And whenever I see the movie paycheck with, uh, Ben Affleck. Okay. This whole, he was, his job in this movie was they would find this equipment, these people, this companies would make, right? He come in and his job was reverse engineer all this stuff and figure out what, how everything ticked out worked. And then he would make improvements and they rebuild it and they launched a competitor a month later and beat out the, these people. Right. That was his whole entire job in the movie. It was really cool. And, uh, and so when I started thinking about something, it's just like paycheck, man, I got to see this guy's making a million bucks a year right now. I need to be, I need to be this, I need to be Ben Affleck. I need to come in and I need to pull this whole thing apart, see how it all works. When I see how it all works, that I can put it back together and make my own and make it a little bit better and boom, make even more money. Okay. This is really like, I, I, I'd say probably the biggest secret you guys. Some people think, oh, Russell, you're a genius. No, I'm not. I just find out what's working. I reverse engineered and make my own version. Like that's, that's the whole game. Okay. It's very, very simple. Now there are five things I'm looking for and I'm reverse engineering and offer. Okay. An offer or a funnel, whatever it is you want to call it. Okay. These are the five things I'm looking for. First off is the demographics. This is who, who, right? We talked about who, who do they sell them to? Okay. I need to, I need to find out that. Second thing I find out is what's their offer? What are they selling? Are they selling an ebook? They have a free play shipping product. They're selling on a webinar. Like how are, how are they making money? Okay. If it's working for them, it's probably going to work for me as well. So I want to find out exactly what their offer is. A lot of people say, Russell, what should I, what should I sell my product for? What are your competitors selling for? Uh, they're selling for $27 bucks, sell for $27 bucks. Well, how, like why, maybe I should do mine for $37. Well, I wouldn't, like they've proven it works. So let's just, just, just model it, copy it, whatever you want to call it. Like do the same price as they're doing it first. You can, you can tweak and test things later, but the very first time, I'm going to try to get the most perfect funnel as possible from day number one. So I'm going to model every single thing as close as I can do. Okay. Um, you don't want to go and try to, try to reinvent the wheel initially. Okay. So the offer is the second thing. What's the offer? What are they selling? How are they bundling it? Um, I want to know everything about that. Okay. Any of my competitors' videos, sales videos, when I go to their sites, you can ask my brother, I, I go to the sales video and I send him an email. He's a video guy. I said, Scott, I want you to go and rip the audio out of this, out of this sales video, here's the audio. And I send that audio to a transcriptionist and someone transcribes the whole sales video and I print it out and I have it on my computer and I read through that thing a thousand times. I want to know exactly what they're offering. Okay. I know someone else who does that too. He's knitting on his head back there. Right. That's how it works. Okay. I've got literally every single sales video in every single market I'm in, print it out. Everyone Ryan Dice's offers, I have them every front end video, upsells, downsells, printed out on my, on my computer. I didn't know exactly what's happening at any given second and all the offers in my market. Okay. So that, the first thing is the front end offer. Now you have to buy your competitor's product. Okay. Okay. How much money it costs you need to buy it? How many times did you buy a nurse cell? Uh, 12. How many times did you buy it on purpose? Uh, three times on purpose. Okay. Three times on purpose. Like literally I'm, I'm obsessed with this stuff. If you guys have read through 108 split test winners, like if you've seen, like we'd make little tiny tweaks to, to a color or to a button and things like that and you'll see a hundred, 200% increase just by tweaking something. What's his look like exactly? I'm going to model it and I can test from there, but my, I want my best guess going out day number one, right? Let's say I can't find the ad copy. I can still reverse engineer pretty close, but there's two unknowns. I can't find two things. Then I'm usually not going to model that thing. I want to make sure I know as much as humanly possible about every single aspect of that. Where should I sell for? I don't know. Let's find out what these guys are selling for. They're selling for 47 bucks. You know what? I bet you they've tested that price. So I could either go and test it or I could just copy them because that's what they've proven has worked. Right? I was listening to, anyone here know Agora Publishing? They're a billion dollar information company. They're probably the biggest info publishers in the world. And they sell financial newsletters and they sell financial newsletters, $100 a year to join their financial newsletters. They do this in the eight different divisions and do over a billion dollars selling these financial newsletters. And I heard Porter Sandsbury who runs their biggest division, he used to, he's worked there, I think he retired or something. But I heard him speak on stage and he said, it was interesting, he said, hey, if you're going to do a financial newsletter, you should sell it for $97 a year. He said, do you know how I know that? He said, we tested everything else in the world and the only way we've ever made it successful is $97 a year. He said, you can come out and do every one. You can sell it for $50 a year because you're going to undercut it. You can sell it for $200 a year if you think you can make more money. But we've tested things so well in over a billion dollars in sales. This is the only thing we'll ever do. So why would I try to second guess that? That's the price people are paying. It's just like they've done all the testing for you. Venus Factors done testing for me. I'm going to sell my product at $47. If this is the way I want to do it. So I'm going to buy this product. I'm going to see what the upsells are, I'm going to see what the downsells are. I'm going to find out exactly what this offer is. That's key. That gives me number two. It also gives me number three. I saw what their landing page was. So the next thing I got to find out is where is their traffic source? Where is $100,000 a day of money coming from? We're going to cover a couple more of them tonight, but the process we go through. So there's, and I'm glad you asked this question, because this leads to it. So there's two ways to buy traffic through this concept. What I'm showing you guys right now, what this is showing me, this is showing me basically, we call it media buying, which is banner ads. Okay. This is reverse engineering, for most part banner ads. Like what sites are they buying banner ads on? Okay. This is not going to show me what email lists they're buying emails from. This is not going to show me a bunch of other stuff. It's just going to show me the banner ads. Okay. So this is what we're talking about first is the first kind of traffic that we're going to be targeting is banner ads. Okay. Banner ads. Now, when you do this, there's basically, there's two ways to do it. Okay. One is you go direct to the site. The other way is you go through a network. Okay. Now the network, the concept behind the network is basically, is there's these dudes who basically set up, they got access to like a ton of sites, right? Like thousands of sites. Like buysellads.com has access to thousands. Google's on, Google ads on thousands of sites, right? And so what the concept is you go to Google ad to Google and you're going to go and you're going to buy ads on all these tons and tons of sites. Now in theory, it's awesome. The problem is that most of you guys will lose, including me, will lose our shirts going directly to a network. Okay. I won't teach you guys how to do this because we don't do it right now because it's basically the network you're putting your ad in there. It's going on a thousand sites and you're testing all these things, all these variables and guess what happens? Your money starts getting spent so, so, so, so fast. Like literally we tested this, we did this with NERSA. We had a guy who's, this professional guy who's super good at ad networks. We put in an ad and within one day he spent $20,000 of our money. In one day and it was not profitable. They do what he's doing. He's like, well you told me to put in networks and I put it in and I thought you were awesome. This is like I am. Like well then why is it not working? He's like, I don't know. He's like how to work on your side. I'm like I don't know. All the traffic came in in literally 24 hours and I was done and nothing I could do about it. Okay. And that's what happened to networks is you're testing a thousand sites, a thousand pages on all this stuff and it's to make that profitable you've got to be a ninja. Okay. Now I'm not a ninja yet. I'm working someday I'll become a ninja. So I don't do this. What I do is I go direct because I can test small and I can find out what's going to work and not going to work. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to go directly initially to these sites and I'm going to try to buy an ad directly from that site owner. So I'm going to go to these sites. I'm going to find the ones like healthpages.org and she clicked on it which I think it might be a network. Mr. Movie Times, Vintage and then I'm going to do some research on four or five things. I'm going to try to find like 10 sites or so that I think would be awesome to buy direct from right. I'm going to find 10 sites. If you're most I'm in contact with those 10 sites all mostly sites. If you scroll the very, very bottom there's going to be like there all their footer links to the bottom right has like their terms of service and all that kind of stuff. Usually there's a little link down there that says advertise. You click on that link and there'll be a page and usually they'll have some kind of media kit or something and they'll tell you all about their site. So like here's our site. Here's our demographics. Here's a bunch of stuff. If you're interested in advertising with us give us a call or send us an email whatever and all you do is basically you send an email to that site and you start a dialogue with whoever it is on their side that sells ads. Okay. Very low tech. That's why I like this kind of stuff. It's not like high tech. So I go to that site and I email all 10 people and now I'm going to start using this to bid against each other, right? Because I want to get the best rate. So email is tended for people and my goal right now is to try to find like all I really want to do is I want to buy ads on three sites. Okay. And the reason why if you do one site and it fails you have no idea if it failed because the site sucked or because your ad sucked or because you have no idea why. So I never like this to gamble on one thing. Okay. I like to have three different sites I'm going to do my initial test on. Okay. The difference is in network you're testing a thousand sites. See how like how much risk there is in that? Like okay put on a thousand sites. I have no idea which one's going to work. In 90% there's going to be failures and there's going to be like maybe three or four percent that are going to work. I don't know what that is. It's like I wasted 20 or 30 grand. We're here. I'm going to do my best guess effort to find out exactly which sites are going to work. I'm going to contact 10 of them and use that to negotiate. Like well these guys are going to get to me for this price and this price. I negotiate down to the point where I have three sites I'm going to test with and I'm going to buy ads on those three sites. Okay. And it's not a difficult process. And all of a sudden done basically come back to me like hey I'm going to buy an ad on your site. What I want to do is I'm willing to spend X. I'm only spending a thousand dollars or 500 dollars or whatever your number is and like you got to kind of figure out what your budget is going to be on this. Let's say your budget is a thousand dollars. So I'm going to take that thousand dollars and try to split between these three sites. Some sites won't let you do it that low. Other sites will let you but you're going to try to negotiate that to get in there and then you put your banner out on these three sites and you can start testing it and traffic will start coming and really quick you're going to find out if it's working. Okay. And if one site's working or two or none of them and all this stuff will start happening and that's when you start finding out all these things. Okay. It was the demographics right. Did the offer work? Did the landing page work? Did the traffic source work? All these things start happening and that's how we start buying sites direct. Does that make sense? Okay. Now when I'm doing this again like I said we're modeling everything as close as we can. So I know that this is what their offer looks like right here. That's what their landing page looks like. I know that this is what their banner ads look like. So I'm making and I know these are the sites that they're buying these banner ads on. So I'm coming back and I'm replicating making a campaign that looks similar. It's on a similar site and the banner ads similar. And after I've got one site working then we add another one and we keep growing until we get five sites and then ten sites and every single one of these things when you add it in it just starts growing the traffic that's coming into your site. And, uh, come back. So with email, basically two core things that we do with, with email. Okay. I talked to you guys about the strategy of building your own email list. I think you guys all get that pretty, pretty strong. So the next thing is, is just like you, if you got a list of a hundred thousand people and you send the email, you're going to make money. There are tons of people out there in almost every single market you can dream of have email lists the exact same way. And almost all of them, well, you do one of two things. Number one, you can come up to those guys and say, Hey, you got a big huge email list. How would you like to send an email out and sell my product and I'll pay you a commission on each of those, right? Pretty basic. We call it an affiliate or a joint venture partner. Okay. My very first product ever created. Um, I didn't have a list. I don't know how to drive traffic, but I met this guy, he had email list like 15,000 people. I was like, Hey man, this is my product. Um, I'll give you a hundred percent commission to sell it. He was like, why would you give me a hundred percent commission? I'm like, I don't know. You should just do it. And in my head, I was like, amateurs, folks on the front end, you got a hundred percent to sell it. So he sent an email out to his list. He sold a whole bunch of them. I gave him all the money he made from it. And guess what I got? 217 subscribers. I might as well make a big deal to you guys, but that was the beginning of my, my business. I had, I had people, I had an audience now. Now all this attractive character stuff. I have someone I can like give it to like, Hey guys, I'm awesome. This is why it all, some of these little group of people started liking me. And I went to the next thing I'm like, Hey, um, you got a big email. This is my product, converts really, really good. I'll give you a hundred percent to promote it. Right. All right. I'm sure they're thinking to me like, what a moron. Like, you don't make any money when you give a hundred percent away, but amateurs focus on the front end, right? I understood that. So they promote it and boom, I was, and I got, they, they, they promote my offer, get all the money, but I got a list. And I kept doing that. I kept doing that. And eventually my list was a thousand people and then 5,000 and 10,000. Now we're over a half a million people. Okay. And, uh, and that's kind of, uh, one really good strategy. Now you don't always have to give an affiliate person a hundred percent, but most people who get in this business, they err on the other side and they're like, I'm going to pay my affiliate 10%. Okay. There's not many affiliates who get excited about 10%. And if they don't promote your thing, then guess what you get? You get nothing. Okay. If our goal is our initial funnel to break even, give them a hundred percent, give them 50%, give them 80, whatever that number is, give them as much as they want because your goal is not to make money initially. Your goal is to build a list so you can make money on everything else. Right. So affiliates are, are a great way to do that. Now a lot of people, they don't do affiliate things for whatever reason. Okay. Um, and so for those people I come back to them and say, Hey, well, you don't do affiliate stuff. Well, can I buy an ad in your list? How about I write an ad? I give you a whole bunch of money and, uh, you just send an email out to that list and, uh, and I keep all the money. And for whatever reason, a ton of people like doing this. It's crazy. Uh, we call them solo ads. Okay. So what we normally do is, um, in each market, not every market, but a lot of markets, there's like list brokers that work in those markets and, uh, and basically come to them and they kind of negotiate on your half or you go directly to the person you're trying to negotiate, but it changes a lot. Um, Brent, what Brent's on our team, the buys all the ads, what would you say is like an average, what's a, what's a good average we give people on? What's cool about it is like, like he was saying, like the, the more specific you're getting, the more expensive you're going to do, but if you can create an offer that's more mass appeal. So like our ner, our neuropathy is nerve pain, right? A lot of people have nerve pain, uh, weight loss. A lot of people want to lose weight, make money. A lot of people want to, want to make money. And so the, you know, again, everyone's like, I don't want to go in the hyper competitive markets, but the hyper competitive markets are the best. Cause I can buy for a lot cheaper, a bigger list. And if it, if it appeals to more people, it's going to be cheaper for me and get more people in the funnel. The more specific you are, the more expensive it is. Um, but you can also target and get that. Like we, like our health related lists, we twice as much money as like, uh, like a general list, but usually on the health related lists, they have health problems and we'll make way more money. So it just, it, it kind of plays with that. How do you find a list broker? I would guess you go to Google and type in list broker, um, or just ask, ask around. Um, we've worked with, uh, list brokers in the bizop space. So like Dagen Smith's great in the bizop space. He runs a site called the home business agency. It basically fuels like every MLM out there. Um, in the personal development space, we use a guy, personal development also like, um, uh, like survival lists and kind of that market is growing. Um, another guys we use is, uh, is, uh, Jack Whalen. What's their company name or Jim Whalen? Sorry. Um, is there, is that the company name is Jim Whalen or is it James Whalen agency? So there's a lot of different list brokers. So we use them direct. A lot of times though, there's not list brokers. Like when we first started doing a nurse, so we were looking for diabetes lists. There's not like, at least I haven't found like a broker who just deals with diabetes lists. So I just went into Google. I literally typed in, in Google typed in diabetes, uh, advertising email, something like that. And on top 10 page, there's like three or four people that were like, we have diabetic lists, there's our advertising rates and all that stuff came up. And just like, just like, um, with a banner ads, you go direct to the site as opposed to working through, through a network. But, um, I would say for a lot of you guys, especially if, if you're like me and you're impatient, you set the whole funnel up and you're like, okay, what's the fastest way to test this? The fastest way is email. Like let's, let's gamble 500 bucks or a thousand bucks and just see if it's going to work and you put it in there. And what's cool, like I said, is you'll find out within hours. A lot of times. Like when we first did our, in January, we did our first test with a new, new neuro self-funnel. Uh, the ad went out at what time, Brent? Do you think it was like five or six in the morning? So by the time we got the office, we showed up and Brent said, it was like 10 AM, he's like, do we just broke even? I'm like, what? My 10 AM is like, yeah, we just broke even. I'm like, holy crap, this is going to be awesome. And it's been a ride ever since then. Okay. Cause by 10 AM, we'd already broken even. And after that, I think we tripled over that first day. We're like, this is great. And we started just running. So you never know what's going to happen until you test it. Now we made it like another example, um, in our, in our weight loss market, the very first funnel we built out. So our first upsell OTO stands for one time offer. So our first one time offer, we had 25% conversion. Our second off offer, we got 19% conversion and our third offer, we got 16% conversion. Here's how to drink chocolate and make, and lose tons of weight. Like something like that that's sexy on the front end. And so it's just fixing that piece, trying it out again and see what happens. That makes sense you guys. So that's why I love this game. Because that's what it is. You create this funnel the best you possibly can. You buy some ads and you look at it and it either works out of the gate. You break even, you're like awesome. Break even, what could you do now to sell people on the back end? Or it doesn't work, you look at it and you're like, what, what sucked about this? And if you can find and identify it, then you just tweak that, try again. And within two or three tries, usually we get it right. Okay. And so that's, hopefully that'll give you guys some encouragement. If you're in this process and something hasn't worked yet, if it doesn't work, it doesn't mean it's dead. It means there's something broken. You just got to find that little piece. And as soon as you get that to work, everything else can start working for you. So this question was before ClickFunds, what do you use to track it from these pages? We were really unsophisticated. All we did is we looked at how many clicks we got. So in our click account, we got a thousand clicks, let's say. How many options I got? I got 380 opt-ins. How many people bought that? One dude bought that. How many people bought this? You know, whatever that is, I just looked at it. It wasn't like some fancy, there's no like super good software. It's just like, here's the whole thing. ClickFunds will do that now. The other thing, this is a side note from a testing standpoint, which is kind of cool, is when we send these emails out, and this is the same on banner ads and stuff like that, like just changing like your headline on your banner ad or the subject line of your email can change the whole campaign as well. So when Brent bought the solo ad for this, he said, what headlines do you want to test? And again, all these tests are in the split test book. We put different headlines in, like one of them was, it's not, excuse me, it's not your fault. They're trying to make you, or they're making you fat or something like that, conspiracy theory based, a couple of other ones. One of the headlines I threw, and it's like a joke, we put in four headlines, one of them was, Carrie's dirty little weight loss secret. Okay. And anyway, we send the email out, and what was interesting is that email, the first email that gets subject line, you know, let's say the email goes out to 100,000 people. Person number one gets the headline number one, number two, and it rotates through and it starts over, right? So when all of a sudden done, you know, 250,000 or 200, whatever, a fourth of all those people got a different headline. And then we looked at the stats and we found out that, so here's the email back here, look at the stats, that it was like, the four different headlines, one of the headlines had like six times more clicks than the other ones, six times more clicks. And so I looked at it and I was like, man, if that headline went to every email, we've got an extra 2,000 clicks for free that we didn't get because the headline didn't capture people's attention. So the second email we sent out, we just used that, which increased the throughput on everything. Now what was cool is we found out Kerry's dirty weight loss secret, that that got people excited to open an email and click, right? So we did, we came back here now to the landing page, we changed the headline that we had before to find out what Kerry's dirty little weight loss secret is, and guess what, this shot up, more people opted in. And then the headline on this is like, hey, this product's gonna teach you Kerry's dirty little weight loss trick, and conversions went up there. So you can learn these little things along the way that you can take back and just, you find out this subject line works best, let's move that throughout the entire funnel. So his question was, if you find out email on subject line works better or worse, how long do you wait to repeat the campaign? The reality is, as soon as Brent tells me I can try again, because it's not like, we're buying $5,000 apps, he's like, you don't get another one this month. I'm like, all right, in the next month, can I try again? Yeah, you can try it. That's part of it. The reality is like, especially if you, I know this from doing joint ventures and stuff, I can send email to my list promoting a product, I can send an email the next day promoting a product and get just as much or more money the second day as I did the first day. So you could, if you can just write the check a couple of times, you could do it really, really quick and really quick and easy. I don't think the saturation happens that nearly. I mean, we did Dr. Suzanne's list we did for NeuroCell and we did it once and it crushed it. We did like a week later and crushed it again. So like, you can, you can usually do it pretty quick. People's attention span is really short, especially if you're tweaking the subject line. Different people are going to respond. Some people, it's just, email is interesting because none of us read all of our emails every single day. So everyone that's getting it, there's only a fraction of people that get it every day anyway and you're always hitting different people in different times and there's, it'll usually work multiple times before it stops working.
